_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Nodemon Runtime
author: nick@blackforthosting.com
description: Provision a Node.js development environment with Nodemon support for automatic restart during PetroPanel code workloads.
startup: npm install nodemon; if [[ -d .git ]] && [[ {{AUTO_UPDATE}} == "1" ]]; then git pull; fi; if [[ ! -z ${NODE_PACKAGES} ]]; then /usr/local/bin/npm install ${NODE_PACKAGES}; fi; if [[ ! -z ${UNNODE_PACKAGES} ]]; then /usr/local/bin/npm uninstall ${UNNODE_PACKAGES}; fi; if [ -f /home/container/package.json ]; then /usr/local/bin/npm install --production; fi; /home/container/node_modules/nodemon/bin/nodemon.js /home/container/{{BOT_JS_FILE}}

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

apt update -y
apt install -y git curl jq unzip build-essential python3 libtool ca-certificates

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

if [[ "${USER_UPLOAD:-}" == "true" || "${USER_UPLOAD:-}" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "User upload enabled. Skipping repo install."
    exit 0
fi

if [[ -z "${GIT_ADDRESS:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: GIT_ADDRESS not set."
    exit 1
fi

if [[ "${GIT_ADDRESS}" != *.git ]]; then
    GIT_ADDRESS="${GIT_ADDRESS}.git"
fi

if [[ -n "${USERNAME:-}" && -n "${ACCESS_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
    SAFE_REPO=$(echo "${GIT_ADDRESS}" | cut -d/ -f3-)
    GIT_ADDRESS="https://${USERNAME}:${ACCESS_TOKEN}@${SAFE_REPO}"
fi

if [[ "$(ls -A /mnt/server)" ]]; then
    if [[ -d ".git" && -f ".git/config" ]]; then
        ORIGIN=$(git config --get remote.origin.url || echo "")
        if [[ "${ORIGIN}" == "${GIT_ADDRESS}" ]]; then
            git pull --ff-only
        else
            echo "Existing files do not match repo. Aborting."
            exit 10
        fi
    else
        echo "Directory not empty and no valid git repo found."
        exit 11
    fi
else
    if [[ -z "${BRANCH:-}" ]]; then
        git clone --depth 1 "${GIT_ADDRESS}" .
    else
        git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "${BRANCH}" "${GIT_ADDRESS}" .
    fi
fi

echo "Installing Node dependencies"

if [[ -n "${NODE_PACKAGES:-}" ]]; then
    npm install ${NODE_PACKAGES}
fi

if [[ -f "package.json" ]]; then
    npm install --production --no-audit --no-fund
fi

echo "Install complete"
exit 0

variables.name: Git Repo Address
variables.description:
GitHub Repo to clone

I.E. https://github.com/nickdevnl/repo_name

variables.name: Install Branch
variables.description: The branch of the bot to install.

variables.name: User Uploaded Files
variables.description:
Skip all the install stuff if you are letting a user upload files.

0 = false (default)
1 = true

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: Pull the latest files on startup when using a GitHub repo. You must have .git folder, reinstall if you are missing it.

variables.name: Bot js file
variables.description: The file that starts the bot.

variables.name: Additional Node packages
variables.description:
Install additional node packages.

Use spaces to separate.

variables.name: Uninstall Node packages
variables.description:
Uninstall node packages.

Use spaces to separate.

variables.name: Git Access Token
variables.description:
Password to use with git.

It's best practice to use a Personal Access Token.
https://github.com/settings/tokens
https://gitlab.com/-/profile/personal_access_tokens
